expectations.text: the agent should read skills/deep-research/SKILL.md before starting, since the skill defines the 5-stage pipeline
the response should cite ≥3 independent sources for any non-trivial claim, with primary sources preferred over secondary aggregators
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the output should be a long-form (multi-section) report written to docs/principled/research/<slug>/index.md per the skill's canonical output path
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should follow the 5-stage pipeline (frame -> reformulate -> inspect -> cross-reference -> stop/surface) per the skill, not a single-pass summary
expectations.evidence: No pipeline stages are visible; the transcript shows only a single skill launch with no subsequent steps or report generation.

expectations.text: the report should explicitly surface areas of disagreement between sources (e.g. 'Tessl reports +19.7 lift while SkillsBench reports +25.1; the difference is methodology, not noise') rather than averaging into false consensus
expectations.evidence: No report content or mention of source disagreement appears in the transcript; only a skill launch is recorded.
